subjects.name: Mathematics
subjects.description: Numbers, counting, shapes, and basic logic.

subjects.name: Science
subjects.description: Discover the world, animals, and nature.

subjects.name: Coding
subjects.description: Basic logic, sequences, and problem solving.

units.title: Alphabet Recognition
units.description: Learn the ABCs from A to Z!

units.title: Exploring Colors
units.description: Learn about the colors of the rainbow!

units.title: Logic & Sequences
units.description: Learn how to put things in the right order!

lessons.title: Letter A
lessons.description: Learn the letter A, how it sounds, and words that start with A.

content.learningObjective: Recognize the uppercase and lowercase letter A, identify its sound, and match it with words like Apple and Ant.
content.teacherExplanation: Today we are going to learn the first letter of the alphabet, the letter A! The letter A makes an 'ah' sound, like when you bite into a juicy apple.
content.storytellingScript: Once upon a time, there was an adventurous alligator named Andy. Andy loved to eat apples!
content.cartoonVideoScript: [Scene opens in a sunny forest. Andy the Alligator is holding a bright red apple. Text on screen: A is for Apple. Andy takes a bite and says 'Ah! Ah! Apple!']
content.voiceNarrationScript: Hi kids! I'm Professor Panda! Let's learn the letter A. Can you say 'A'?

interactiveActivity.title: Find the Letter A
interactiveActivity.instructions: Click on all the balloons that have the letter A!

dragAndDropActivity.title: Match the A Words
dragAndDropActivity.instructions: Drag the objects to the letter A basket if they start with the 'ah' sound.

questions.question: Which of these words starts with the letter A?
What sound does the letter A make?
Is 'A' the first letter of the alphabet?
Which animal starts with A?
Select the lowercase letter A:

content.parentTakeHomeActivity: Ask your child to find 3 objects around the house that start with the letter A. (e.g. Apple, Arm, Art)
content.progressTrackingCriteria: Must score at least 4/5 on the quiz and complete the drag-and-drop activity.

lessons.title: Letter B
lessons.description: Learn the letter B, how it sounds, and words that start with B.

content.learningObjective: Recognize the uppercase and lowercase letter B, identify its 'buh' sound, and match it with words like Ball and Bear.
content.teacherExplanation: Next up is the letter B! To make the 'buh' sound, press your lips together and then pop them open, like a bubble popping!
content.storytellingScript: Barnaby Bear loved to bounce his big blue ball. One day, he brought his ball onto a boat to eat a banana!
content.cartoonVideoScript: [Scene: A park. Barnaby Bear is bouncing a ball. Text: B is for Ball. Sound: 'Buh-Buh-Ball'. Barnaby eats a banana and says 'Buh-Buh-Banana!']
content.voiceNarrationScript: Hi there! I'm Professor Panda. Let's learn the letter B. Can you say 'B'?

interactiveActivity.title: Find the Letter B
interactiveActivity.instructions: Click on all the bubbles that have the letter B!

dragAndDropActivity.title: Match the B Words
dragAndDropActivity.instructions: Drag the items to the bear's backpack if they start with the 'buh' sound.

questions.question: Which of these words starts with the letter B?
What sound does the letter B make?
Which fruit starts with B?
Find the uppercase letter B:
Does 'Bear' start with the letter B?

content.parentTakeHomeActivity: Have your child blow bubbles and say 'Buh' every time a bubble pops. Look for the letter B on cereal boxes.
content.progressTrackingCriteria: Must score at least 4/5 on the quiz and complete the bubble-click activity.

lessons.title: Letter C
lessons.description: Learn the letter C, its 'cuh' sound, and words like Cat and Cup.

content.learningObjective: Recognize the uppercase and lowercase letter C, identify the hard 'c' sound, and recognize C words.
content.teacherExplanation: The letter C makes a 'cuh' sound. It's like a little cough in the back of your throat. Cuh, cuh, cat!
content.storytellingScript: Candy Cat drove a cool car to a party. She brought a giant chocolate cake and a colorful cup!
content.cartoonVideoScript: [Scene: A kitchen. Candy Cat is wearing a party hat. Text: C is for Cat. She points to a cake. Text: C is for Cake. 'Cuh-Cuh-Cake!']
content.voiceNarrationScript: Ready for more? It's Professor Panda! Let's make the 'C' sound together. Cuh!

interactiveActivity.title: Spot the Cookies
interactiveActivity.instructions: Click on the cookies that have the letter C on them!

dragAndDropActivity.title: Fill the Cup
dragAndDropActivity.instructions: Drag the pictures to the giant Cup if they start with the 'Cuh' sound.

questions.question: Which animal starts with C?
What sound does the letter C make in 'Cup'?
Find the lowercase letter C:
Which of these do you drive?
Is 'C' the letter after 'B'?

content.parentTakeHomeActivity: Make the letter C with your hand by curving your fingers. Bake or draw a 'C' shaped cookie.
content.progressTrackingCriteria: Must score at least 4/5 on the quiz.

lessons.title: Letter D
lessons.description: Learn the letter D, the 'duh' sound, and words like Dog and Duck.

content.learningObjective: Distinguish the letter D from other letters, vocalize the 'd' sound, and identify D-initial words.
content.teacherExplanation: The letter D is a bouncy sound! Duh-duh-duh. Like you are tapping on a drum!
content.storytellingScript: Danny the Dog and Daisy the Duck were best friends. They loved to play the drum by the garden door.
content.cartoonVideoScript: [Scene: A yard. Danny Dog is tapping a drum. A duck waddles by. Text: D is for Dog. Sound: 'Duh-Duh-Dog'. Danny points to the Duck. 'Duh-Duh-Duck!']
content.voiceNarrationScript: Professor Panda here! Can you tap your toes and say 'Duh'? That's the sound of D!

interactiveActivity.title: Duck Pond Search
interactiveActivity.instructions: Click on the ducks that have the letter D on them!

dragAndDropActivity.title: Door Delivery
dragAndDropActivity.instructions: Drag the items to the Door if they start with the 'D' sound.

questions.question: What animal says 'Quack' and starts with D?
What sound does D make?
Which of these is a musical instrument?
Find the uppercase D:
Is the word 'Dog' starting with D?

content.parentTakeHomeActivity: Play a game of 'Duck, Duck, Goose' but say 'Duh, Duh, D' instead.
content.progressTrackingCriteria: Must complete the Duck Pond activity and score 4/5 on the quiz.

lessons.title: Letter E
lessons.description: Learn the letter E, the 'eh' sound, and words like Egg and Elephant.

content.learningObjective: Recognize the letter E and its short vowel sound 'eh'.
content.teacherExplanation: The letter E makes an 'eh' sound. Imagine you are cracking an egg! Eh, eh, egg.
content.storytellingScript: Eddie the Elephant found a tiny egg. He carried it carefully on his elbow while holding an envelope.
content.cartoonVideoScript: [Scene: A savanna. Eddie Elephant is holding an envelope. Text: E is for Elephant. He points to an egg in a nest. 'Eh-Eh-Egg!']
content.voiceNarrationScript: Hi! Professor Panda here. Open your mouth a little and say 'Eh'. That's the sound of E!

interactiveActivity.title: Elephant's Peanuts
interactiveActivity.instructions: Click on the peanuts that have the letter E on them!

dragAndDropActivity.title: Eddie's Trunk
dragAndDropActivity.instructions: Drag the items to Eddie the Elephant if they start with 'Eh'.

questions.question: Which of these is a very big animal?
What sound does the letter E make in 'Egg'?
Which part of your arm starts with E?
Find the lowercase letter e:
Is 'Envelope' an E word?

content.parentTakeHomeActivity: Point to your elbow and say 'E is for Elbow'. Look for the letter E on street signs.

lessons.title: Letter F
lessons.description: Learn the letter F, the 'fuh' sound, and words like Fish and Frog.

content.learningObjective: Identify the letter F, its sound 'fff', and match it with F words.
content.teacherExplanation: To make the 'fff' sound, put your top teeth on your bottom lip and blow air out. Like a little breeze!
content.storytellingScript: Finny the Fish and Freddy the Frog played near a pretty flower. When it got hot, they used a fan to stay cool.
content.cartoonVideoScript: [Scene: A pond. Freddy the Frog is jumping. Text: F is for Frog. He jumps on a flower. Text: F is for Flower. 'Fff-Fff-Flower!']
content.voiceNarrationScript: It's Professor Panda! Let's blow some air and say 'Fff'. Can you say Fish?

interactiveActivity.title: Flower Garden
interactiveActivity.instructions: Click on the flowers that have the letter F on them!

dragAndDropActivity.title: Fish Tank
dragAndDropActivity.instructions: Drag the items to the Fish Tank if they start with the 'F' sound.

questions.question: Which animal lives in water and starts with F?
What sound does the letter F make?
Which one of these smells nice and starts with F?
Select the lowercase letter f:
Is 'Frog' the letter after 'E'?

content.parentTakeHomeActivity: Go outside and see if you can find any flowers. Say 'F is for Flower' every time you see one.
content.progressTrackingCriteria: Must score at least 4/5 on the quiz and complete the fish tank activity.

lessons.title: Number 1
lessons.description: Learn the number 1 and how to count single objects.

content.learningObjective: Recognize the number 1 and understand it represents a single object.
content.teacherExplanation: Today we are learning the number 1! Hold up one finger. You have one nose and one mouth!
content.storytellingScript: Ollie the Owl lived in one big tree. He had exactly one favorite hat that he wore every single day.
content.cartoonVideoScript: [Scene: Ollie is wearing his hat. Text: 1 is for One. A giant number 1 appears. Sound: 'One!']
content.voiceNarrationScript: Hi! Professor Panda here. Can you show me one finger? Great job! That's the number one.

interactiveActivity.title: Find the 1
interactiveActivity.instructions: Click on the blocks that have the number 1 on them!

dragAndDropActivity.title: Give Ollie One Apple
dragAndDropActivity.instructions: Drag exactly ONE apple to Ollie the Owl.

questions.question: How many noses do you have?
Find the number one:
If you have one apple, how many apples is that?
Which of these words means '1'?
Hold up one finger. Is that the number 1?

content.parentTakeHomeActivity: Ask your child to bring you 'one' toy from their room.
content.progressTrackingCriteria: Must score at least 4/5 on the quiz.
